title: FedDynamics: Dynamic Client Participation with HL7/FHIR Integration
abstract: This paper introduces FedDynamics, a novel framework for handling dynamic client participation in federated learning environments, with specific application to healthcare systems through HL7/FHIR integration. We address challenges of client churn, intermittent connectivity, and regulatory compliance in healthcare data exchange. Experimental results demonstrate improved convergence rates and model performance in unstable network conditions compared to traditional federated learning approaches.

title: BlockFed: Incentive Mechanisms for Federated Learning via Blockchain
abstract: BlockFed proposes a blockchain-based incentive mechanism to encourage high-quality participation in federated learning systems, addressing the challenge of incentivizing participants. We implement a reputation and token-based system that rewards clients based on the quality and quantity of their contributions. Our approach uses smart contracts to ensure transparency and fairness in reward distribution, while maintaining privacy of model updates. Evaluation shows increased participant engagement and improved global model performance compared to non-incentivized approaches.

title: FedSplit: Efficient Partitioning Strategies for Federated Model Training
abstract: FedSplit presents algorithms for efficient model partitioning in federated learning settings, allowing computationally constrained devices to participate in training. Our approach splits neural network models into components that can be distributed across clients with varying computational capabilities, enabling participation of resource-constrained devices. We demonstrate that this approach not only expands the pool of potential participants but also improves training efficiency and reduces communication overhead. Experiments on image classification and natural language processing tasks show competitive accuracy while reducing computation requirements by up to 75%.

title: PrivacyFL: Enhanced Differential Privacy for Federated Learning
abstract: PrivacyFL introduces refined differential privacy mechanisms specifically designed for federated learning scenarios, providing improved privacy-utility tradeoffs. We develop adaptive noise calibration techniques that dynamically adjust privacy parameters based on data sensitivity and model convergence state. Our framework includes client-specific privacy budgets and heterogeneous noise addition, accommodating varying privacy requirements across participants. Extensive evaluation on benchmark datasets demonstrates that PrivacyFL achieves up to 15% higher accuracy than standard differentially private federated learning approaches at equivalent privacy guarantees.

title: FedVision: Federated Learning for Computer Vision Applications
abstract: FedVision tackles the challenges of applying federated learning to computer vision tasks, addressing issues of data heterogeneity, model convergence, and communication efficiency. We propose specialized techniques for vision tasks including adaptive aggregation for convolutional layers, feature alignment methods to handle non-IID image distributions, and gradient compression tailored for vision models. Experiments on image classification, object detection, and segmentation tasks demonstrate that FedVision achieves centralized-equivalent performance with significantly reduced communication overhead and improved convergence speed.

title: CrossFed: Cross-Silo Federated Learning in Healthcare Consortia
abstract: CrossFed establishes protocols for cross-silo federated learning among healthcare institutions, ensuring regulatory compliance while enabling collaborative learning. We address the unique challenges of federated learning in healthcare settings, including data governance, accountability, and regulatory requirements like HIPAA and GDPR. Our framework includes audit mechanisms, differential privacy guarantees, and flexible participation policies. Evaluation across five major healthcare systems shows that CrossFed enables effective collaboration while maintaining compliance, leading to improved diagnostic models for rare conditions where any single institution would have insufficient data.

title: Decentral and Federated Machine Learning in Edge Devices
abstract: Federated machine learning defines a framework that allows a collective model to be constructed from distributed data repositories. It provides guidelines for decentralized Machine Learning, utilizing computational power of IoT, mobile, and edge devices without data leaving the respective device, thus ensuring privacy by design. Yet, simple Federated Learning Frameworks naively assume an honest central server. The survey covers data partitioning, privacy preservation, optimization, local model transportation, personalization, motivation mechanisms, platforms, and benchmarks.

title: Federated Learning in Edge Computing: A Systematic Survey
abstract: This paper surveys the implementation of Federated Learning (FL) in edge computing, covering data partitioning, privacy preservation, model optimization, local model transportation, personalization, motivation mechanisms, platforms, and benchmarks. Economic impacts and a manufacturing paradigm using FL are discussed, alongside future research directions.

title: Combined Federated and Split Learning for Ubiquitous Intelligence in IoT
abstract: This article reviews federated and split learning in edge computing for IoT, highlighting state-of-the-art methodologies, applications, and future directions.

title: NeuroCuts: Neural Network for Optimizing Packet Classification
abstract: Packet classification is a fundamental problem in computer networking. NeuroCuts utilizes neural networks to encode state and action space and efficiently explores decision trees to optimize global objectives such as classification time and memory usage.

title: A Survey on Model-Heterogeneous Federated Learning
abstract: Questo articolo esamina gli approcci all'avanguardia nel federated learning eterogeneo a livello di modello, analizzando i punti di forza e di debolezza e identificando le sfide aperte e le direzioni future della ricerca.

title: Advances in Robust Federated Learning: Heterogeneity Considerations
abstract: Questo articolo delinea i concetti fondamentali del federated learning eterogeneo e riassume le sfide della ricerca in termini di dati, modelli, compiti, dispositivi e comunicazione.

title: Dynamic Heterogeneous Federated Learning with Multi-Level Prototypes
abstract: Questo studio introduce un nuovo framework di federated learning chiamato Federated Multi-Level Prototypes (FedMLP) per affrontare scenari dinamici con distribuzioni di dati eterogenee tra diversi client.

title: Architectural Blueprint For Heterogeneity-Resilient Federated Learning
abstract: Questo articolo propone una nuova architettura a tre livelli per il federated learning, ottimizzata per ambienti di edge computing, che affronta le sfide legate all'eterogeneità dei dati dei client e ai vincoli computazionali.

title: Federated Learning for Computationally-Constrained Heterogeneous Devices: A Survey
abstract: Questo sondaggio fornisce una panoramica completa delle sfide e delle soluzioni nel federated learning per dispositivi eterogenei con vincoli computazionali.

title: Noise-Robust Federated Learning With Model Heterogeneous Clients
conference: IEEE Transactions on Neural Networks and Learning Systems
abstract: Questo articolo presenta una soluzione innovativa che gestisce simultaneamente l'eterogeneità dei modelli e il rumore delle etichette in un unico framework.

title: An Efficient Personalized Federated Learning Approach in Heterogeneous Environments
abstract: Proponiamo FedPRL, un nuovo approccio al federated learning personalizzato progettato specificamente per ambienti eterogenei.

title: Hierarchical Over-the-Air Federated Learning with Awareness of Interference and Data Heterogeneity
abstract: Questo lavoro introduce un metodo di apprendimento progettato per affrontare le sfide dell'eterogeneità dei dati e dell'interferenza nei network wireless, insieme a uno schema di trasmissione scalabile che utilizza efficientemente una singola risorsa wireless attraverso il calcolo over-the-air.

title: FLASH: Federated Learning Across Simultaneous Heterogeneities
abstract: Questo lavoro propone FLASH, un algoritmo di selezione dei client leggero e flessibile che supera i framework FL all'avanguardia sotto fonti estensive di eterogeneità, bilanciando le informazioni statistiche associate alla qualità dei dati del client, alla distribuzione dei dati e alla latenza.

title: FedPMVR: Addressing Data Heterogeneity in Federated Learning with Momentum-Based Partial Variance Reduction
abstract: Questo articolo propone una nuova tecnica di riduzione della varianza parziale basata sul momentum per mitigare gli effetti negativi dell'eterogeneità dei dati nel federated learning, regolando gli aggiornamenti del gradiente per i layer di classificazione finali della rete neurale del client.

title: Federated Learning in Dynamic and Heterogeneous Environments
abstract: Questo lavoro esplora tecniche avanzate per l'adattamento dinamico dei modelli e la gestione di dati eterogenei in scenari di edge computing, proponendo soluzioni innovative per migliorare la robustezza e l'efficienza del federated learning.

title: Federated Learning in Practice: Reflections and Projections
abstract: Questo articolo riflette sulle pratiche attuali del federated learning, proponendo un quadro ridefinito che dà priorità ai principi di privacy e tracciando un percorso futuro attraverso l'uso di ambienti di esecuzione fidati e ecosistemi open-source.

title: AI-Based Container Orchestration for Federated Cloud Environments
abstract: Questo articolo propone un'architettura per l'orchestrazione di container basata su intelligenza artificiale in ambienti di cloud federato, migliorando l'efficienza e la scalabilità del federated learning.

title: Adaptive Federated Learning for Resource-Constrained IoT Devices
abstract: Questo studio presenta una strategia efficiente di offloading dei microservizi mirata a ottimizzare i costi in ambienti di Mobile Edge Computing (MEC) cloud eterogenei.

title: On-Demand Model and Client Deployment in Federated Learning with Deep Reinforcement Learning
abstract: Questo lavoro propone una soluzione on-demand per il deployment di nuovi client utilizzando Docker Containers, affrontando la disponibilità dei client e la selezione in ambienti dinamici e mobili tramite l'uso del Deep Reinforcement Learning.

title: Implementing Federated Learning in Healthcare: Best Practices
abstract: Questo articolo discute le migliori pratiche per l'implementazione del Federated Learning nel settore sanitario, enfatizzando l'adozione di standard come HL7 FHIR per garantire uno scambio dati senza soluzione di continuità tra diversi sistemi e istituzioni.

title: Federated Systems for Automated Infection Surveillance: A Perspective
abstract: Questo articolo esplora l'utilizzo di sistemi federati per la sorveglianza automatizzata delle infezioni, sottolineando come lo standard HL7 FHIR rappresenti un'evoluzione significativa per l'interoperabilità dei dati sanitari.
